subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can identify the environmental conditions that help living things grow well.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can choose one electronic and one non-electronic way to send information.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can explain how different organs work together as a body system.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can name and explain the steps of Earth's water cycle, like evaporation and rainfall.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can look at test results to understand how well different solutions work.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can observe and explain how two objects move after they collide, whether they have the same or different weights.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can decide which solutions best solve a problem.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can show how moving energy moves from one object to another.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can explain how changes in temperature affect the energy of water molecules and how water changes between solid, liquid, and gas.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can explore and name the main parts of a cell and what they do.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child understands that the choices people make can cause different results.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can ask questions about everyday machines or tools at home or school.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can ask questions about how strong magnetic forces are.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can compare where fossils and rocks are found and how continents fit together to show how Earth's plates have moved.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child understands that waves with bigger heights carry more energy.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can recognize situations where quick reactions help living things survive.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can identify traits that parents can pass on to their children.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can use weather data to compare conditions in different places on the same day.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child will help design a way to keep heat inside a substance or container.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can investigate and identify features that living things inherit from their parents.
subject: Science

description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between things that are alive and things that are not.
subject: Science

description_plain: This standard is combined with SES-MS-PS1-6.
